'''Alex Walker''' (born 4 September 1995)<ref name="The Rugby World Cup leaves rugby league fans with mixed emotions">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/no-helmets-required/2015/oct/21/rugby-world-cup-league-fans-mixed-emotions|title=The Rugby World Cup leaves rugby league fans with mixed emotions|publisher=Guardian|date=2015-10-15|accessdate=2016-02-08}}</ref> is a [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|fb}} for the [[London Broncos]] in the [[RFL Championship|Betfred Championship]].

He has previously played for the [[London Broncos]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] and the [[Super League]], and spent time on loan from the [[London Broncos|Broncos]] at the [[London Skolars]] and the [[Hemel Stags]] in [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]].

==Background==
Walker was born in [[Harlow, Essex]], England.<ref name="The Rugby World Cup leaves rugby league fans with mixed emotions"/>

Walker is a product of the London Broncos academy system and made his first team debut on 13 September 2014. Before joining London Broncos Academy, Walker played for Brentwood Eels RLFC as a youth player.

==Playing career==
===London Broncos===
Walker made his début for the [[London Broncos]] in the [[Super League]] against the [[Bradford Bulls]] in 2014.<ref name="Super League: London Broncos 36-46 Bradford Bulls">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/29133502|title=Super League: London Broncos 36-46 Bradford Bulls|publisher=BBC|date=2014-09-14|accessdate=2016-01-29}}</ref>

Walker played a pivotal role in the Broncos' successful 2018 season, the culmination of which was their 4-2 win over the [[Toronto Wolfpack]] in the [[Million Pound Game]] to earn promotion to Super League in 2019.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/toronto-vs-london/50049 |title=Toronto 2-4 London: Broncos shock Wolfpack to earn 2019 Super League place |website=Sky Sports |date=8 October 2018 |access-date=31 July 2024}}</ref>

===Featherstone Rovers (loan)===
On 3 August 2021 it was reported that he had signed for [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on loan until the end of the 2021 season.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=3 Aug 2021|accessdate=12 Aug 2021|title=Featherstone sign Alex Walker on loan from Wakefield|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/featherstone-sign-alex-walker-on-loan-from-wakefield/}}</ref>
He scored a try on his debut against the [[Batley Bulldogs]] on 8 Aug 2021.

===Halifax Panthers===
On 12 November 2021 it was reported that he had signed for the [[Halifax Panthers]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=12 Nov 2021|accessdate=24 Dec 2021|title=Alex Walker joins Championship club on trial|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/alex-walker-joins-championship-club-on-trial/}}</ref>

===London Broncos re-sign===
In February 2022, Walker returned to London Broncos on a deal until the end of the season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/london-broncos-sign-scotland-international-alex-walker |title=London Broncos re-sign Scotland international Alex Walker |website=Love Rugby League |date=25 February 2022 |access-date=31 July 2024}}</ref>
On 15 October 2023, Walker played in the London Broncos upset [[Championship Grand Final]] victory over [[Toulouse Olympique]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67105947|title=Championship Grand Final: Toulouse 14-18 London Broncos - Capital club back in Super League|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>

===International===
Walker made his international début for [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] in 2015.<ref name="European Championship: Scotland v Ireland preview">{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/scotland-vs-ireland/preview/44352|title=European Championship: Scotland v Ireland preview|publisher=BBC|date=2015-10-23|accessdate=2016-01-29}}</ref>
[[File:Alex Walker.jpg|thumb|right|Walker playing for Scotland in 2015]]
